idea.hook: How many stars are in OUR galaxy?
facts: Our Milky Way galaxy contains an estimated ~200 BILLION stars.
The Observable Universe is home to an estimated ~2 TRILLION galaxies.
That means there are TEN TIMES more galaxies in the Universe than stars in our entire home galaxy!
idea.payoff: Each one a cosmic island, filled with billions of untold stars, making the true number of stars unimaginable.
idea.title: Stars in OUR Galaxy vs. Galaxies in Universe?! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: How many stars are in OUR galaxy?
scenes.visual: A stylized question mark composed of shimmering stars, slowly rotating against a dark cosmic background.
A vivid, animated spiral galaxy (representing the Milky Way) forms and zooms into view, glittering with countless tiny points of light.
The single galaxy visual transitions to a vast cosmic field, filled with an unimaginable number of tiny, diverse galaxy shapes, slowly drifting and sparkling.

scenes.text: 10x MORE Galaxies in Universe than stars in Milky Way!
scenes.visual: A large, bold '10x' symbol dominates the screen, surrounded by many small galaxy icons, contrasting with a single, small Milky Way icon.

scenes.text: Each a cosmic island... untold billions of stars. Truly unimaginable.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, slowly panning shot through a deep field of galaxies, revealing more and more distant, beautiful cosmic structures, emphasizing the immense scale of the universe.

youtube.title: Stars in OUR Galaxy vs. Galaxies in Universe?! 🤯🌌
